Description (Card): As an admin, I want to register/update/delete users so that buyer and seller accounts can be managed in the system.
Acceptance Criteria (Confirmation):
Developer needs to input the following information from the console using programming.
Display the menu to:

1. To register new user - if option 1 is entered, then get the User attributes as mentioned below, create a User object and add to ArrayList<User>:
  - userId: a string data type (minimum 5, maximum 20 characters, alphanumeric only)
  - fullName: a string data type with a maximum length of 50 characters
  - email: a string data type with a maximum length of 100 characters (must contain @ and domain)
  - mobileNumber: a string data type (exactly 10 digits)
  - password: a string data type (minimum 8 characters, must contain at least one uppercase, one lowercase, one digit, and one special character)
  - city: a string data type (e.g., Delhi, Mumbai, Bangalore, Chennai, Hyderabad, Pune)
  - role: a string data type (must be either 'buyer' or 'seller')
Once all fields are taken as input and validated, create a User object and insert it to the ArrayList.
Before inserting, check if userId or email already exists in the ArrayList. If duplicate found, display: 'User ID already exists' or 'Email already registered'.
After successful registration, display: 'User Registration is successful. Welcome <fullName>!'

2. To Update the User details - if option entered is 2, then get the userId and the field to update (email, mobileNumber, or city). Update the value for the specific userId.
Once the user object is updated, display: 'User details updated successfully for <userId>'.
If userId is not found, display: 'User not found'.

3. To Delete the user - if option entered is 3, get the userId and remove the related user from the ArrayList.
Once identified and removed, display: 'User <userId> has been deleted successfully'.
If userId is not found, display: 'User not found'.

4. To View all users - if option entered is 4, display all users in a formatted table showing: userId, fullName, email, city, role.

* This maps to Frontend US001 (Registration) and SQL US001/US018 (Users table and authentication queries).

Description (Card): As a buyer/seller, I want to login to the system using my credentials so that I can access role-specific features.
Acceptance Criteria (Confirmation):
Developer needs to implement login simulation from the console.

Display the login prompt:
- Enter User ID: (read from console)
- Enter Password: (read from console)

Validation Logic:
- Iterate through the ArrayList<User> to find a matching userId and password combination.
- If credentials match, store the logged-in user reference in a currentUser variable.
- Check the role of the matched user:
  a) If role is 'buyer', display: 'Login successful. Welcome <fullName> (Buyer)!' and show the Buyer Menu.
  b) If role is 'seller', display: 'Login successful. Welcome <fullName> (Seller)!' and show the Seller Menu.
- If no match is found, display: 'Invalid User ID or Password. Please try again.'
- After 3 consecutive failed attempts, display: 'Account locked. Please restart the application.'

Buyer Menu:
1) Browse Available Cars
2) View Car Details
3) My Wishlist
4) Recently Viewed Cars
5) Purchase a Car
6) My Purchase History
7) Compare Cars
8) Recommendations
9) Logout

Seller Menu:
1) View My Listings
2) Add New Car
3) My Sales History
4) View My Reviews & Rating
5) Seller Dashboard
6) Logout

* This maps to Frontend US002/US003 (Login) and SQL US018 (Authentication queries).

Description (Card): As a seller, I want to add/update/delete car listings so that I can manage my inventory on the marketplace.
Acceptance Criteria (Confirmation):
Developer needs to input the following information from the console.
Display the Seller Car Management menu:

1. Add New Car - if option 1 is entered, get the Car attributes as mentioned below, create a Car object and add to ArrayList<Car>:
  - carId: auto-generated integer (increment from last carId in ArrayList)
  - make: string (must be one of: Maruti, Hyundai, Honda, Toyota, Tata, Mahindra, Kia, Ford, Volkswagen, BMW)
  - model: string (maximum 30 characters)
  - year: integer (must be between 2000 and current year)
  - price: double (must be between 25000 and 10000000)
  - mileage: integer (must be between 0 and 500000)
  - fuelType: string (must be one of: Petrol, Diesel, CNG, Electric)
  - transmission: string (must be one of: Manual, Automatic)
  - color: string (White, Black, Silver, Red, Blue, Grey)
  - sellerId: auto-set to currentUser's userId
  - status: default 'available'
  - listingDate: current date (use java.time.LocalDate)
  - viewCount: default 0
  - healthScore: auto-calculated using formula: 100 - (currentYear - year) * 3 - (mileage / 10000) * 2, clamped between 0 and 100

After adding, display: 'Car listed successfully! Listing ID: <carId>. Health Score: <healthScore>'
Also run fraud detection: if price is more than 40% below the average price of cars with the same make and year in the ArrayList, display: 'WARNING: This listing has been flagged for unusual pricing.'

2. Update Car Price - if option 2, get carId and new price. Update only if the car belongs to the current seller.
Display: 'Price updated for Car ID: <carId>'

3. Remove Car Listing - if option 3, get carId and remove from ArrayList only if status is 'available' and belongs to current seller.
Display: 'Car listing <carId> removed successfully'

4. View My Listings - if option 4, display all cars where sellerId matches currentUser in a formatted table: carId, make, model, year, price, status, healthScore, viewCount.

* This maps to Frontend US008 (Add Car) and SQL US002/US003 (Cars table).

Description (Card): As a buyer, I want to browse all available cars with filtering and sorting options so that I can find cars matching my needs.
Acceptance Criteria (Confirmation):
As a Buyer, when the option for 'Browse Available Cars' is selected from the Buyer Menu, display all cars from ArrayList<Car> where status is 'available'.

Display each car in a formatted row showing: carId, make, model, year, price, fuelType, transmission, healthScore, viewCount.

After displaying all cars, show the Smart Filter sub-menu:
1) Filter by Price Range - prompt for minPrice and maxPrice, display cars within range
2) Filter by Make/Brand - prompt for make name, display matching cars
3) Filter by Fuel Type - prompt for fuel type (Petrol/Diesel/CNG/Electric), display matching cars
4) Filter by Year Range - prompt for fromYear and toYear, display cars within range
5) Filter by Transmission - prompt for Manual/Automatic, display matching cars
6) Combined Filter - prompt for price range, fuel type, and year range together, display cars matching ALL criteria
7) Sort by Price (Low to High) - display sorted ArrayList
8) Sort by Price (High to Low) - display sorted ArrayList
9) Sort by Health Score (Highest First) - display sorted ArrayList
10) Sort by Newest Listings - sort by listingDate descending
11) Back to Main Menu

Display count: 'Showing X available cars' before each result.
If no cars match the filter, display: 'No cars found matching your criteria.'

* This maps to Frontend US006 (Car Listing with Smart Filters) and SQL US011 (Smart Search queries).

Description (Card): As a buyer, I want to view complete details of a specific car including health score and seller rating so that I can make an informed decision.
Acceptance Criteria (Confirmation):
As a Buyer, when the option for 'View Car Details' is selected, prompt: 'Enter Car ID:'

Retrieve the car from ArrayList<Car> matching the carId.
If not found, display: 'Car not found.'

If found, display the following sections:

1. Car Information:
  - Make, Model, Year, Color
  - Price (formatted with commas)
  - Fuel Type, Transmission
  - Mileage (formatted, e.g., 45,000 km)
  - Status: Available/Sold
  - Listed on: <listingDate>

2. Health Score:
  - Display calculated score (0-100)
  - Display grade: 'Excellent' (70-100), 'Good' (40-69), 'Poor' (0-39)
  - Display breakdown: 'Age Deduction: -X points | Mileage Deduction: -X points'

3. Price Analysis:
  - Calculate average price of all cars with the same make and year from ArrayList
  - Display: 'Market Average for <make> <year>: ₹X,XX,XXX'
  - Display: 'This car is X% below/above market average'
  - If price is more than 40% below average, display: 'FRAUD ALERT: Price is significantly below market average!'

4. Seller Information:
  - Find seller from ArrayList<User> using car's sellerId
  - Display: Seller Name, City, Role
  - Calculate average rating from ArrayList<Review> for this seller
  - Display: 'Seller Rating: X.X/5 (Y reviews)'
  - Display: 'Total Cars Sold: Z' (count from ArrayList<Purchase>)

5. People Also Viewed:
  - Find and display 3 cars from ArrayList with same fuelType or price within ±15%, excluding current car
  - Display: carId, make, model, price, healthScore

After viewing, increment the car's viewCount in the ArrayList.
Also add this carId with current timestamp to the buyer's recentlyViewed list (ArrayList<CarView>).

* This maps to Frontend US007 (Car Details) and SQL US009/US010/US012 (Health Score, Fraud, Recommendation queries).

Description (Card): As a buyer, I want to purchase a car so that the transaction is recorded and the car is marked as sold.
Acceptance Criteria (Confirmation):
As a Buyer, when the option for 'Purchase a Car' is selected, prompt: 'Enter Car ID to purchase:'

Retrieve the car from ArrayList<Car>.
If not found, display: 'Car not found.'
If car status is 'sold', display: 'Sorry, this car has already been sold.'

If car is available, display Order Summary:
- Car: <make> <model> <year>
- Price: ₹<formatted price>
- Seller: <seller name>
- Health Score: <score> (<grade>)

Prompt: 'Select Payment Method: 1) Cash  2) Bank Transfer  3) EMI'
If EMI is selected, display EMI breakdown at 9% annual interest:
- 12 months: ₹<amount>/month
- 24 months: ₹<amount>/month
- 36 months: ₹<amount>/month
EMI formula: P * [r(1+r)^n] / [(1+r)^n - 1] where r = 0.0075 (monthly rate)

Prompt: 'Confirm purchase? (yes/no)'
If 'yes':
  a) Create a Purchase object with: purchaseId (auto-increment), carId, buyerId (currentUser), sellerId, purchaseDate (current date), amount, paymentMethod
  b) Add to ArrayList<Purchase>
  c) Update car status to 'sold' in ArrayList<Car>
  d) Remove car from buyer's wishlist (ArrayList<Wishlist>) if present
  e) Display: 'Purchase Successful! Transaction ID: <purchaseId>'
  f) Prompt: 'Rate this seller? (1-5):' and optional comment, create Review object and add to ArrayList<Review>

If 'no', display: 'Purchase cancelled.' and return to Buyer Menu.

* This maps to Frontend US012 (Purchase Car) and SQL US004/US016 (Purchases table and history).

Description (Card): As a buyer, I want to add, remove, and view cars in my wishlist so that I can save cars for later.
Acceptance Criteria (Confirmation):
As a Buyer, when the option for 'My Wishlist' is selected from the Buyer Menu, display the Wishlist sub-menu:

1. View My Wishlist - Display all cars in the buyer's wishlist:
  - Iterate through ArrayList<Wishlist> where userId matches currentUser
  - For each entry, find the car from ArrayList<Car> using carId
  - Display: carId, make, model, year, price, healthScore, status (available/sold), addedDate
  - Show total count: 'Your Wishlist: X cars'
  - If wishlist is empty, display: 'Your wishlist is empty.'

2. Add Car to Wishlist - Prompt: 'Enter Car ID to add:'
  - Check if car exists in ArrayList<Car>
  - Check if car is already in buyer's wishlist (prevent duplicates). If duplicate, display: 'This car is already in your wishlist.'
  - If valid, create a Wishlist object with: wishlistId (auto-increment), userId, carId, addedDate (current date)
  - Add to ArrayList<Wishlist>
  - Display: '<make> <model> added to your wishlist!'

3. Remove Car from Wishlist - Prompt: 'Enter Car ID to remove:'
  - Find and remove the entry from ArrayList<Wishlist> where userId matches currentUser and carId matches input
  - Display: 'Car removed from your wishlist.'
  - If not found, display: 'Car not in your wishlist.'

4. Back to Main Menu

* This maps to Frontend US009 (Wishlist Page) and SQL US005/US013 (Wishlist table and operations).

Description (Card): As a buyer, I want to view my recently viewed cars so that I can quickly return to cars I was browsing.
Acceptance Criteria (Confirmation):
As a Buyer, when the option for 'Recently Viewed Cars' is selected from the Buyer Menu:

Retrieve all CarView entries from ArrayList<CarView> where userId matches currentUser.
Sort by viewedAt timestamp in descending order (most recent first).

Remove duplicate carIds, keeping only the most recent entry for each car.
Display the top 10 most recently viewed cars in a formatted table:
- carId, make, model, year, price, fuelType, healthScore, status, viewedAt

For each entry:
- Find the car from ArrayList<Car> to get current details
- Display availability: 'Available' or 'Sold'

Show count: 'Recently Viewed: X cars'
If no cars have been viewed, display: 'You have not viewed any cars yet.'

Sub-menu after display:
1) View details of a car (enter carId) - calls the View Car Details logic
2) Add a car to wishlist (enter carId)
3) Back to Main Menu

* This maps to Frontend US010 (Recently Viewed) and SQL US007/US014 (CarViews table and queries).

Description (Card): As a buyer, I want to see personalized car recommendations so that I can discover cars matching my preferences.
Acceptance Criteria (Confirmation):
As a Buyer, when the option for 'Recommendations' is selected from the Buyer Menu:

Logic for generating recommendations:

1. If the buyer has recently viewed cars (ArrayList<CarView> has entries for this user):
  - Get the last viewed car's details from ArrayList<Car>
  - Find all available cars where:
    a) fuelType matches the last viewed car's fuelType AND
    b) price is within ±20% of the last viewed car's price AND
    c) carId is not the last viewed carId
  - Display up to 3 matching cars as 'Recommended for You'

2. If no viewing history exists (fallback):
  - Display the 3 most recently listed available cars (sorted by listingDate descending)
  - Display heading: 'Newest Listings for You'

3. Top Rated Sellers' Cars:
  - Calculate average rating per seller from ArrayList<Review>
  - Find available cars from sellers with average rating >= 4.0
  - Display up to 3 such cars as 'Cars from Top-Rated Sellers'

Display each recommended car showing: carId, make, model, year, price, healthScore, sellerRating.

If no recommendations can be generated, display: 'No recommendations available at this time. Browse all cars to get started!'

* This maps to Frontend US004 (Recommendation Section) and SQL US012 (Recommendation queries).

Description (Card): As a seller, I want to view my ratings and reviews so that I can track my reputation on the platform.
Acceptance Criteria (Confirmation):
As a Seller, when the option for 'View My Reviews & Rating' is selected from the Seller Menu:

Retrieve all reviews from ArrayList<Review> where sellerId matches currentUser's userId.

Display Seller Rating Summary:

Description (Card): As a seller, I want to view my dashboard with performance metrics so that I can track my sales performance.

As a Seller, when the option for 'Seller Dashboard' is selected from the Seller Menu:

Calculate and display the following metrics by iterating through ArrayLists:

3. Rating Summary:
  - Average Rating from ArrayList<Review> for this seller
  - Total Reviews count
  - Display: 'Rating: X.X/5 (Y reviews)'

4. Active Listings Detail:

  - Display: 'Most Popular Listing: <make> <model> (<viewCount> views)'

Description (Card): As a buyer, I want to view my purchase history with summary analytics so that I can track all my car purchases.
Acceptance Criteria (Confirmation):
As a Buyer, when the option for 'My Purchase History' is selected from the Buyer Menu:

Retrieve all purchases from ArrayList<Purchase> where buyerId matches currentUser.

Display Purchase Summary:
- Total Cars Bought: count of purchases
- Total Amount Spent: sum of all purchase amounts, formatted with ₹ and commas
- Most Recent Purchase: date of the latest purchase
- Display: 'Total Purchases: X | Total Spent: ₹X,XX,XXX | Last Purchase: <date>'

Display Each Purchase (sorted by purchaseDate descending):

Sub-menu:
1) View details of a purchased car (enter purchaseId) - show full car details
2) Rate a seller (enter purchaseId) - check if review already exists in ArrayList<Review> for this purchaseId. If exists, display: 'You have already reviewed this seller for this purchase.' If not, prompt for rating (1-5) and optional comment, create Review and add to ArrayList.
3) Back to Main Menu

If no purchases exist, display: 'You have not purchased any cars yet.'

Description (Card): As a buyer, I want to compare multiple cars side by side so that I can evaluate which car offers the best value.
Acceptance Criteria (Confirmation):
As a Buyer, when the option for 'Compare Cars' is selected from the Buyer Menu:

Maintain a temporary ArrayList<Integer> comparisonList (max 3 car IDs) during the session.

Display Compare Cars sub-menu:
1. Add Car to Compare - Prompt: 'Enter Car ID:'
  - Validate car exists in ArrayList<Car>
  - Check if comparisonList already has 3 cars. If full, display: 'Maximum 3 cars can be compared. Remove one first.'
  - Check for duplicates. If already added, display: 'Car already in comparison list.'
  - Add carId to comparisonList. Display: '<make> <model> added to comparison.'

2. Remove Car from Compare - Prompt: 'Enter Car ID to remove:'
  - Remove from comparisonList. Display: 'Car removed from comparison.'

3. View Comparison - Display only if comparisonList has >= 2 cars:
  - Display a side-by-side comparison table with columns for each car:
    Row 1: Make & Model
    Row 2: Year
    Row 3: Price (formatted)
    Row 4: Fuel Type
    Row 5: Transmission
    Row 6: Mileage
    Row 7: Health Score (with grade)
    Row 8: Seller Rating
    Row 9: Fraud Flag (FLAGGED/NORMAL)
    Row 10: Price vs Market Avg (X% above/below)
  - Highlight best value per row in console using [BEST] marker:
    Lowest price, lowest mileage, highest healthScore, highest sellerRating
  - If fewer than 2 cars, display: 'Add at least 2 cars to compare.'

4. Clear All - Empty the comparisonList. Display: 'Comparison list cleared.'
5. Back to Main Menu

* This maps to Frontend US011 (Car Comparison Page) and SQL US009/US010/US017 (Health Score, Fraud, Price queries).

Description (Card): As a system, I want to automatically detect potentially fraudulent car listings so that buyers are protected from scams.
Acceptance Criteria (Confirmation):
The fraud detection logic should run automatically when:
a) A new car is added to ArrayList<Car> (in US003)
b) A buyer views car details (in US005)

Detection Logic:
1. Group all cars in ArrayList<Car> by make and year
2. Calculate the average price for each group
3. For the target car, compare its price to the group average:
  - If price < average * 0.60 (more than 40% below), mark as FLAGGED
  - Otherwise, mark as NORMAL

Implementation:
- Create a method: String detectFraud(int carId) that returns 'FLAGGED' or 'NORMAL'
- Create a method: double getMarketAverage(String make, int year) that calculates average price from ArrayList
- Create a method: double getPriceDeviation(int carId) that returns the percentage deviation

Console Output when flagged:
'⚠ FRAUD ALERT: Car ID <carId> (<make> <model>) is priced at ₹<price> which is <X>% below the market average of ₹<avgPrice> for <make> <year>.'

When running a full scan (Seller Dashboard or Admin), iterate through all available cars and list all flagged listings.

* This maps to Frontend US006/US007 (Fraud Warning) and SQL US010 (Fraud Detection query).

Description (Card): As a system, I want to track car view counts so that popular listings can be identified and displayed.
Acceptance Criteria (Confirmation):
View count tracking should be integrated into the View Car Details flow (US005).

Logic:
1. Every time a buyer views a car's details, increment the viewCount field on the Car object in ArrayList<Car>.
2. Create a CarView object with: viewId (auto-increment), userId, carId, viewedAt (current datetime) and add to ArrayList<CarView>.

Popularity Features:
1. Most Viewed Cars - When accessed from any menu:
  - Sort ArrayList<Car> by viewCount descending (status = 'available' only)
  - Display top 5 most viewed cars: carId, make, model, price, viewCount
  - Display: 'Most Popular Cars on the Platform:'

2. View Count Display:
  - Show viewCount alongside car details in all listing displays
  - Format: '<viewCount> views' next to each car

3. Trending Cars:
  - Count views from ArrayList<CarView> in the current session
  - Display cars with the most views in the current session as 'Trending Now'

* This maps to Frontend US006 (View Count) and SQL US007/US014 (CarViews table and popularity queries).

Description (Card): As a user, I want to submit feedback or report issues so that my concerns are recorded in the system.
Acceptance Criteria (Confirmation):
As a User (Buyer or Seller), when 'Contact Support' option is selected from either menu:

Display Support Information:
- Phone: +91-1800-XXX-XXXX (Toll Free)
- Email: support@usedcarmart.com
- Working Hours: Mon-Sat, 9 AM - 6 PM IST

Display Feedback sub-menu:
1. Submit Feedback - Prompt for:
  - Subject: display options (1: General Inquiry, 2: Report a Bug, 3: Report Fraud, 4: Feedback, 5: Other)
  - Message: string input (minimum 20 characters, maximum 500 characters)

2. View My Feedback History - Display all feedback submitted by currentUser from ArrayList<Feedback>:

3. Back to Main Menu
